examples.input: A 39-year-old male reports that for the past six months, a right mandibular posterior tooth has been sore and weak when biting on hard food. He has no history of spontaneous pain. On examination, the occlusal surface shows partial enamel attrition with exposed dentin. The exposed dentin is extremely tender to probing. A carious lesion, tender to probing, is found in the buccal groove. The tooth is sensitive to cold stimuli. The diagnosis for the affected tooth related to the chief complaint is ____.
If the two abutment teeth are in good condition, using a bridge to restore two missing teeth can generally restore ____ of the original occlusal surface area.
If hypersensitivity pain occurs some time after a full crown has been cemented, the main cause is unlikely to be ____.
Thymic stromal lymphopoietin (TSLP) has a similar structure to which of the following cytokines____.

A patient presents with progressive expansion of the mandibular molar region, angle, and ramus on one side, with a "ping-pong ball" sensation on palpation. Radiographs show a multilocular, cystic radiolucency with highly variable locule sizes and a scalloped margin. The most likely diagnosis is ____.

A 45-year-old male is found on physical examination to have clusters of yellowish-white, miliary-sized spots on the mucosa of the right oral commissure. He is asymptomatic, and similar lesions are also present on the vermilion of the upper lip. The most likely diagnosis is ____.

A 12-year-old female has had swelling in the right maxillary canine region for six months. An X-ray shows a well-defined radiolucency with radiopaque calcifications. A biopsy of the solid mass shows tumor cells arranged in duct-like structures or solid nests, and rosette-like structures are also seen. The most likely pathological diagnosis is ____.

A 60-year-old female presents with bilateral erosion at the corners of her mouth for one month. Clinical examination reveals an edentulous mouth with reduced vertical dimension. The skin and mucosa at both oral commissures are hyperemic, macerated, and fissured. To determine the etiology of this condition, the following examinations should be performed, except for ____.

A 49-year-old patient presents with pain upon biting in the maxillary right posterior tooth, which has persisted for 3 months. Intense pain is elicited when biting at a specific position. Examination reveals occlusal attrition on the maxillary right first molar with dentin exposure. A suspected crack line is observed extending from the central occlusal surface to the mesial aspect. The most appropriate further diagnostic method is ____.

A 23-year-old patient reports a 3-month history of thermal sensitivity and occasional chewing discomfort in a mandibular right posterior tooth. Three days ago, the patient developed sudden, spontaneous, paroxysmal pain, which is exacerbated by hot and cold stimuli and worsens at night. The patient is unable to localize the specific tooth involved. The most helpful examination method to identify the offending tooth is ____.

A 33-year-old male presents with a mass in the left mental region that has been present for 4 years. He reports a sensation of pulsation within the mass. Examination reveals a raised, purplish-red mass measuring approximately 7 cm $\times$ 6 cm with a beaded appearance. The surface temperature is elevated compared to normal skin. A thrill is palpable, and a blowing bruit is audible upon auscultation. The most valuable ancillary testing for definitive diagnosis is ____.

A 55-year-old female presents with a painless mass in the preauricular region that has been slowly enlarging for 6 years. Palpation reveals a well-circumscribed, mobile, globular mass with a nodular surface. It is of moderate firmness and is not adherent to the overlying skin. The most likely diagnosis is ____.
Given an ANB angle of $0^{\circ}$ and an SN-MP angle of $42^{\circ}$, the most likely type of malocclusion is ____.

A patient presents with chronic recurrent submandibular sialadenitis that has been unresponsive to conservative treatment. A hard mass is palpable in the submandibular region, and a calculus is confirmed at the junction of the duct and the gland. The appropriate treatment is ____.

A 40-year-old female presents with a 6-month history of gingival enlargement in the mandibular anterior region, which compromises esthetics. She reports occasional bleeding upon brushing. Examination reveals mild gingival enlargement on the labial aspect of the mandibular anterior teeth. The gingiva has a resilient consistency and near-normal color. A small amount of supragingival and subgingival calculus is detected. When obtaining the medical history, inquiries should focus on all of the following EXCEPT ____.

A patient presents with multiple missing mandibular posterior teeth, mobile anterior teeth, and significant lingual undercuts. The distance from the floor of the mouth to the gingival margin is 6 mm. The preferred major connector is ____.
Children who underwent cleft palate repair in early childhood frequently exhibit ____ during their growth and development.
Under which of the following conditions is the pitch of the voice raised?

A patient experiences pain to cold and heat following a direct restoration for caries. The pain ceases upon removal of the stimulus, and there is no spontaneous pain. The cause of the thermal sensitivity in this tooth is ____.
A patient presents with a complaint of "difficulty opening the mouth." Examination reveals a maximum mouth opening of 1.5 cm. The degree of limitation of mouth opening is ____.

A 9-year-old female patient with a history of left cleft lip and palate underwent surgical repair before the age of 1. She presents with anterior crossbite and dental malalignment. Radiographic examination reveals an alveolar cleft located between the left maxillary lateral incisor and the deciduous canine. What is the optimal timing for alveolar bone grafting?

If an impression taken with alginate impression material cannot be poured immediately to create a working cast, which of the following methods should be adopted to maintain relative dimensional stability prior to pouring the cast?

An 8-year-old male, described as lively and restless and in good health, presents with an irregular ulcer on the left buccal mucosa in the region of the buccal fat pad. The ulcer is relatively deep, with no significant pain. The most likely diagnosis is ____.

Following a dental filling, a patient presents with persistent, spontaneous dull pain. The pain is localizable and exacerbated by mastication, but there is no provoked pain. This is most likely due to ____.

A 14-year-old female presents with a chief complaint that the interdental papillae of her anterior teeth appear bulbous, soft, and shiny. Given a diagnosis of puberty gingivitis, which of the following treatment measures is INCORRECT?

A 25-year-old male presents with a 3-day history of spontaneous pain in the mandibular left posterior tooth, which is exacerbated by cold and heat. He reports that thermal sensitivity began 6 months ago, followed by recurrent dull discomfort. Examination reveals disto-occlusal caries on the mandibular left first premolar. Probing exposes the pulp and elicits intense pain (++). Tenderness to percussion is equivocal (±). The distal gingival papilla is hyperplastic and extends into the carious cavity. The correct diagnosis and most appropriate treatment are ____.
Infection of which of the following spaces is most likely to cause severe limitation of mouth opening (trismus)?

A 21-year-old female presents with spontaneous pain 3 days after restoration of the maxillary anterior teeth. Examination reveals an all-ceramic crown on the maxillary right central incisor, which is negative to percussion, cold testing, and mobility. The maxillary right lateral incisor exhibits distal proximal caries, is sensitive to probing (+), tender to percussion (+), and positive to cold testing (+). Radiographs show no periapical radiolucency associated with the maxillary right central and lateral incisors. The most likely cause of the pain is ____.

Of a 7 mm premolar extraction space, 6 mm is occupied by the mesial movement of the posterior teeth, and 1 mm is occupied by the distal movement of the anterior teeth. The posterior anchorage is classified as ____.

A 36-year-old male presents with swelling and pain in the left submandibular region during meals. The symptoms gradually subside several hours after eating. Examination reveals erythema and swelling at the orifice of the submandibular duct. Gentle compression of the gland elicits a purulent discharge from the duct opening. Which of the following examinations helps to confirm the diagnosis?
For a small, deeply seated tumor with intact overlying skin and mucosa, the most appropriate biopsy method is ____.

A 60-year-old male presents 6 years post-surgery for tongue carcinoma. He previously underwent radiotherapy to the neck and submandibular region; the dosage is unknown. One month ago, he developed a non-healing mandibular gingival ulcer with localized bone exposure, accompanied by severe lancinating pain in the mandibular region. The most likely diagnosis is ____.
Which of the following statements regarding toxic megacolon is INCORRECT?

An 18-year-old female was admitted with a 1-week history of fever and ecchymoses on both lower extremities. Blood tests revealed a white blood cell count of $3.1 \times 10^{9}/\mathrm{L}$, red blood cell count of $2.86 \times 10^{12}/\mathrm{L}$, hemoglobin of 80 g/L, and platelet count of $30\times 10^{9}/\mathrm{L}$. A bone marrow smear showed a marked proliferation of lymphoblasts, accounting for 80%. Which of the following findings is least likely to be observed in this patient's bone marrow examination?

A 40-year-old patient presents with irregular fever, diarrhea, and progressive weight loss persisting for 3 months. Enlargement of multiple superficial lymph nodes in the cervical and axillary regions was noted 1 month ago. A chest X-ray reveals patchy opacities in the left upper lung. Complete blood count shows: white blood cells $2.8\times10^{9}/\mathcal{L}$, neutrophils 0.92, lymphocytes 0.08, platelets $250\times10^{9}/\mathcal{L}$, and hemoglobin 105 g/L. The most likely diagnosis is ____.
A 65-year-old male receives cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) for cardiac arrest. Which of the following statements is correct?
A 25-year-old male presents with gross hematuria. This indicates that each liter of urine contains ____.

A 55-year-old female presents with axillary lymph node enlargement of 1 month's duration. Lymphoma is suspected. Which of the following characteristics of the enlarged lymph nodes does not support this diagnosis?

An 18-year-old female presents for evaluation after a heart murmur was detected during a physical examination. She reports inability to tolerate strenuous physical activity and is prone to frequent colds, but denies a history of lower extremity edema or nocturnal dyspnea. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 130/80 mmHg and a heart rate of 90 bpm. On auscultation, $S_1$ is diminished and $S_2$ is slightly increased. At the apex (A), a Grade 3/6 regurgitant-like systolic murmur (SM) is heard, radiating toward the left axilla. A systolic click is audible at the left 4th and 5th (L4, 5) intercostal spaces; the click becomes more prominent when standing up from a squatting position. The lungs are clear without dry or moist rales. The liver and spleen are not palpable, and there is no edema of the lower extremities.
Which of the following should be considered first for this case?

A 45-year-old male with a history of lung transplantation three years ago underwent renal transplantation for subsequent renal failure. During the first 18 hours post-operation, his urine output was 1200 ml. However, starting from the 20th postoperative hour, the patient became progressively anuric, accompanied by a rapid elevation in serum creatinine. Which of the following types of graft rejection is he experiencing?

A 45-year-old male presents to the emergency department 2 hours after sustaining blunt abdominal trauma from a steering wheel impact during a motor vehicle accident. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 120/80 mmHg, a pulse rate of 90 bpm, and a respiratory rate of 24 breaths/min. The patient exhibits board-like abdominal rigidity with diffuse tenderness and rebound tenderness across the entire abdomen. An upright abdominal radiograph demonstrates free air under the diaphragm (pneumoperitoneum). Which of the following is the most likely injury?

A 20-year-old patient presents with a one-week history of recurrent hematemesis, melena, and mucocutaneous bleeding. On physical examination, the liver and spleen are not enlarged. Laboratory findings reveal a hemoglobin level of 76 g/L, a white blood cell count of $5.0\times10^9/\mathcal{L}$, and a platelet count of $10\times10^9/\mathcal{L}$. Bone marrow examination shows active proliferation with a myeloid-to-erythroid (M:E) ratio of 5:1. The number of megakaryocytes is increased, with an elevated proportion of immature megakaryocytes. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 30-year-old male with a history of intravenous drug use presents with a 10-day history of burning pain in the mouth and tongue, along with dysphagia. Physical examination reveals a lymph node of approximately 1.5 cm in each of the left and right axillae; the nodes are firm, non-tender, and mobile. The lips appear grayish-white, and scattered, patchy, latex-like white plaques are observed on the dorsal surface of the tongue and buccal mucosa; these plaques are tender and difficult to wipe off. Auscultation reveals coarse breath sounds in both lungs, while the heart and abdomen are unremarkable. A chest X-ray suggests bilateral pulmonary tuberculosis. Microscopic examination of oral secretions identifies fungal hyphae and spores. A diagnosis of bilateral pulmonary tuberculosis was made. Which of the following conditions should be highly suspected in this patient?

A 29-year-old woman presents with a one-week history of gingival bleeding and low-grade fever. Laboratory findings on a complete blood count (CBC) were: WBC $11.3×10^9/L$, RBC $2.6×10^{12}/L$, Hemoglobin 68 g/L (Note: In the US/UK, this is typically reported as 6.8 g/dL), and Platelets $30×10^9/L$. A bone marrow aspiration revealed acute monocytic leukemia (M5).
Which of the following physical signs is the least common in this condition?

A 27-year-old man presents with a 3-month history of left upper quadrant fullness and generalized fatigue. On examination, he has a mildly anemic appearance, slight sternal tenderness, and massive splenomegaly. A complete blood count (CBC) shows: WBC $172\times10^9/L$ with an increase in immature cells, RBC $3.50\times10^{12}/L$, Hemoglobin 90 g/L, and Platelets $78\times10^9/L$. A bone marrow aspirate reveals a markedly hypercellular marrow with 36% myeloid blasts, and increased numbers of metamyelocytes and band neutrophils.
What is the most likely diagnosis for this patient?
A 35-year-old woman is diagnosed with hemolytic anemia. For which of the following causes of hemolytic anemia is glucocorticoid therapy NOT indicated?

A 25-year-old female patient is 10 hours postoperative from a subtotal thyroidectomy for primary hyperthyroidism. She becomes agitated with dyspnea, cyanosis of the lips, and neck swelling. The surgical dressing is dry, and there is no bloody output from the drain.
What is the most appropriate immediate management for this patient?
An 18-year-old female patient is found to have massive proteinuria. This is quantitatively defined as a 24-hour urinary protein excretion of____

A 32-year-old man is hospitalized for a 5-day history of fever, cough with white sputum, and a 2-day history of shortness of breath. He has a history of drug abuse. 
Physical examination reveals a temperature of 38.5℃, pulse of 128/min, respiratory rate of 44/min, and blood pressure of 110/70 mmHg. He is tachypneic with cyanotic lips. No lymphadenopathy is present. Auscultation reveals mild moist rales at both lung bases. 
A chest X-ray shows bilateral interstitial disease. A complete blood count (CBC) shows: WBC 3.8×10^9/L (neutrophils 0.92, lymphocytes 0.08), platelets $250\times10^9/\mathcal{L}$, and hemoglobin 105 g/L. An anti-HIV test is positive.
With a preliminary diagnosis of AIDS, which of the following pulmonary complications is the most likely?

A 58-year-old male with a 10-year history of hypertension is under regular outpatient treatment. His recent blood pressure fluctuates between 180-190/100-110 mmHg. A chest X-ray shows an aortic configuration of the heart, an ECG indicates left ventricular high voltage, and proteinuria (+) is present. The most appropriate diagnosis is ____

A 60-year-old female presents with a 2-week history of dizziness and fatigue, accompanied by progressive weight loss. On physical examination, she has an anemic appearance. A lymph node, approximately half the size of a thumb, is palpable in the left cervical region, and the liver and spleen are palpable 2 fingerbreadths below the costal margin. Laboratory findings show: WBC $5.0\times10^9/\mathcal{L}$, hemoglobin 52 g/L, and platelets $165\times10^9/\mathcal{L}$. Unconjugated bilirubin is 56 μmol/L. A bone marrow examination is consistent with hemolytic anemia. The Coombs test is positive. She showed poor response to 4 weeks of standard glucocorticoid therapy. This patient's condition is most likely secondary to which of the following malignancies?

A 50-year-old male presents with a 3-week history of fever and dyspnea. A chest X-ray reveals a large left-sided pleural effusion. An abdominal ultrasound shows hepatosplenomegaly. A complete blood count shows: WBC $2.0\times10^{9}/\mathcal{L}$, hemoglobin 82 g/L, and platelets $65\times10^{9}/\mathcal{L}$. A bone marrow biopsy reveals anaplastic large cell lymphoma. In the past, this type of lymphoma was often misdiagnosed as which of the following diseases?

A 52-year-old male presents with a 2-3 month history of episodic precordial discomfort following exertion, such as walking upstairs. His resting ECG is within normal limits. Further investigation is needed to establish a definitive diagnosis. Which of the following statements regarding the diagnosis of coronary artery disease (CAD) is correct?
The irritative dry cough that develops in patients on long-term therapy with angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitors (ACEIs) is caused by ____

A 24-year-old female presents with an 8-month history of a progressively enlarging and painful swelling below her right knee. On physical examination, the range of motion of the right knee joint is normal. A tender swelling is noted over the proximal end of the tibia. A plain radiograph reveals a destructive lesion in the proximal tibia, characterized by cortical thinning, a central "soap-bubble" appearance, and no significant periosteal reaction. The most likely diagnosis is ____

A 3-year-old male patient, hospitalized for pneumonia complicated by shock, has a 24-hour urine output of 350 ml and a serum creatinine of 325 μmol/L. He is clinically suspected of having acute tubular necrosis (ATN). The most likely urinary diagnostic finding would be ____

An 18-year-old male presents with a 2-year history of bone pain. Laboratory investigations reveal: urine pH 5.0, glycosuria (++), aminoaciduria (+), and urine phosphate crystals (++). Serum levels are: potassium 3.0 mmol/L, calcium 2.0 mmol/L, glucose 4.6 mmol/L, and chloride 110 mmol/L. Blood pH is 7.35. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 38-year-old male presents with severe upper abdominal pain that started 4 hours ago after heavy alcohol consumption. The pain is relieved by leaning forward. Physical examination reveals epigastric tenderness with mild muscle guarding. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 68-year-old female with a 12-year history of chronic bronchitis and emphysema. Two days ago, after a severe coughing spell, she suddenly experienced left-sided chest pain accompanied by dyspnea and was unable to lie flat. What is the most likely diagnosis?
The grafting method in which small autograft patches are embedded into fenestrated allograft sheets generally requires the autograft patch size and the spacing between them to be ____

A 50-year-old female presents with a swollen and severely painful upper lip for 2 days. On physical examination, a purplish-red, swollen mass with a visible pustule is noted on the central upper lip. The diagnosis is a carbuncle of the lip. Its most dangerous complication is ____

A young patient underwent surgical extraction of a lower right, mesially impacted third molar, a procedure involving bone removal and tooth sectioning. Four days later, the patient develops persistent, severe pain radiating to the ear and temporal region. Examination reveals an empty extraction socket with a grayish-white pseudomembrane covering the surface of the alveolar bone wall. What is the most likely diagnosis?

An 11-year-old girl presents for a dental check-up. She reports no spontaneous pain. An oral examination reveals shallow occlusal caries on the left maxillary first and second deciduous molars. The teeth are non-tender to percussion and non-sensitive to probing. Which of the following is the least appropriate management method?

A patient's mandibular left first molar has a deep mesio-occlusal carious lesion. The tooth is sensitive to probing, is not tender to percussion, and exhibits transient sensitivity to a cold test. If the diagnosis is deep caries, the correct management plan should be ____

A 42-year-old male presents with a 5-day history of severe pain in a maxillary left posterior tooth. He is unable to bite down and reports a sensation of the tooth feeling elongated. If the diagnosis is an acute subperiosteal abscess, which of the following signs would typically be present?

A 70-year-old male reports that pain and swelling in his left anterior mandible, which began 3 weeks ago, have now subsided following treatment with anti-inflammatory medication. On examination, the retained root of the mandibular left lateral incisor is found to be non-mobile. A radiograph reveals a periapical radiolucent area of approximately 3 mm × 5 mm, along with slight external resorption at the root apex. The appropriate treatment to be performed on the day of presentation is ____

A patient presents with a one-day history of spontaneous pain in a right posterior tooth, which is worse at night. An examination reveals a deep mesial carious lesion on the maxillary right second premolar. The key examination method for confirming the diagnosis of the affected tooth is ____

A 21-year-old male presents after his maxillary left central incisor was completely avulsed due to trauma. Which of the following statements regarding the management of this avulsed tooth is incorrect?

A 22-year-old male complains of recent, frequent pain from food impaction in a maxillary left posterior tooth. The tooth is sensitive to cold, hot, sweet, and sour stimuli, but he reports no spontaneous pain. Examination reveals a deep occlusal carious lesion on the maxillary left first molar. The floor of the cavity is sensitive to probing, and there is no tenderness to percussion. Before initiating treatment, the most important factor to determine is ____

A 28-year-old male, who has generalized tetracycline staining due to frequent use of the antibiotic in his childhood, requests a bleaching procedure to improve his dental aesthetics. The most appropriate agent to use for this treatment is ____

A 36-year-old male presents with a one-day history of spontaneous pain in a left posterior tooth, which is worse at night. Examination reveals a deep mesial carious lesion on the maxillary left second premolar. The definitive examination method for confirming the diagnosis of the affected tooth is ____

A 46-year-old female presents with a one-week history of sensitivity to cold and hot stimuli in a maxillary left posterior tooth. Examination reveals a deep carious lesion in the maxillary left second molar, in close proximity to the pulp. The patient experiences significant pain during caries excavation. The preferred initial treatment method should be ____

A 2.5-year-old boy presents with deep caries on the labial surfaces of the four upper incisors and shallow caries on the buccal and occlusal surfaces of the maxillary first primary molars. The child has a habit of drinking milk at night. This condition should be considered as ____.

A 10-year-old patient presents with pain in the anterior teeth provoked by cold stimuli for several days. Examination reveals a deep mesial carious lesion on the maxillary right central incisor. The tooth is non-tender to percussion and shows no mobility. Cold testing elicits transient pain. Complete caries removal results in a cavity floor extremely close to the pulp that is sensitive. Which treatment method should be selected?

A 5-year-old female patient presents with pain in the maxillary anterior teeth provoked by cold and heat for one week, with no spontaneous or nocturnal pain. Examination reveals moderate caries on the mesio-lingual surface of the maxillary left primary central incisor. The lesion is sensitive to probing, the tooth is non-tender to percussion, and there is no pulp exposure. The most appropriate filling material to select is ____.

A 52-year-old male. He was diagnosed with type 2 diabetes mellitus 1 year ago and has been treated with appropriate diet, exercise, and oral metformin 500 mg three times daily. Physical examination reveals a height of 173 cm, a weight of 78 kg, and a blood pressure of 130/90 mmHg. Examinations of the heart, lungs, and abdomen show no abnormalities. Follow-up investigations show a fasting blood glucose of 5.2 mmol/L, and 2-hour postprandial blood glucose levels after the three meals are 11.4 mmol/L, 13.1 mmol/L, and 12.6 mmol/L, respectively. The most reasonable next step in treatment is to ____.
A 16-year-old female presents with elevated blood carotene levels due to excessive consumption of tangerines. The yellow staining of the skin and mucous membranes typically appears first on the ____.

A 45-year-old laborer presents with a 6-year history of epigastric pain. The pain typically occurs before meals and is accompanied by acid regurgitation. Two hours ago, the patient developed severe, persistent epigastric pain radiating to the back. Physical examination reveals a board-like abdomen with marked tenderness and rebound tenderness. The temperature is 38.5°C, and the white blood cell count is elevated with 70% neutrophils. The most likely diagnosis is ____.

A 5-year-old child presents with a 3-month history of paroxysmal headaches. The child was admitted to the emergency department due to sudden severe headache and recurrent vomiting for half a day. Examination reveals: the patient is conscious, pupils are normal bilaterally, and nuchal rigidity is present. Half an hour later, sudden respiratory arrest occurs while cardiac activity remains. The diagnosis is ____.

A 28-year-old male, previously mentally healthy, had just arrived by train on a business trip. He suddenly felt that a war was about to break out because many people were running towards the exit. The most likely symptom is ____.

A 30-year-old female presents with persistent fever for 2 weeks and has a history of congenital heart disease. Physical examination on admission reveals an anemic appearance, a grade 4/6 harsh systolic murmur with thrill at the left sternal border in the 3rd and 4th intercostal spaces, and the spleen is palpable 2 cm below the left costal margin. Blood cultures were positive on two occasions. Three days after admission, she develops sudden onset of dyspnea, chest pain, and repeated hemoptysis. The most likely diagnosis is ____.

A 28-year-old male presents with symptoms recurring annually during the spring and autumn seasons. Examination reveals mucosal hyperemia and edema on sites such as the lips and cheeks, occasionally accompanied by erythema and vesicles. However, the vesicles rupture rapidly, resulting in extensive erosions. The eroded surfaces are covered with copious exudate forming a thick pseudomembrane. The diagnosis for this patient is ____.

A severely decayed tooth was extracted, revealing a mass of tissue attached to the root apex. Microscopic examination is characterized by the infiltration of lymphocytes, plasma cells, and macrophages, and the proliferation of vascular endothelial cells and fibroblasts. Irregular proliferating epithelial islands and foam cells are also observed. The pathological diagnosis is ____.

A 45-year-old female presents with a 2-year history of gingival swelling in the mandibular anterior teeth, accompanied by intermittent discharge of pus and blood, and self-perceived tooth mobility. Examination reveals gingival erythema and swelling. The mandibular central and lateral incisors show degree II mobility, with abundant calculus on the root surfaces. The probing depth is 5 mm, with a small amount of purulent exudate in the pockets. The condition should first be considered as ____.
The replacement of normal bone by fibrous tissue containing metaplastic bone trabeculae is seen in ____.

A 29-year-old male presents with swelling in the anterior palate for 3 months. X-ray reveals a circular radiolucent area in the anterior midline of the palate. Microscopic examination shows that the lining epithelium consists of stratified squamous epithelium and pseudostratified ciliated columnar epithelium. The most likely pathological diagnosis is ____.

The affected tooth presents with a deep carious lesion on the occlusal surface. There is no spontaneous pain, but severe pain occurs upon food impaction. Probing reveals a pulp exposure that elicits severe pain, and there is discomfort on percussion. This indicates ____.
A 15-year-old male patient underwent repositioning and splinting due to a traumatic dislocation of the maxillary left anterior tooth. The duration of splinting should be ____.

A 60-year-old male sustained a skull base fracture due to a car accident yesterday. Two hours ago, he suddenly vomited 300 ml of coffee-ground emesis. The diagnosis should first be considered as ____.
